text: PERSPECTIF REPRESENTATION TEORI DI PERTARUNGAN CIVILISASI MASYARAKAT TENGGER This article studies the civilisation dynamic of Tengger community life to struggle in order to preserve their culture in the face of Islamic spreading influence and Indonesian government power. Researches in this paper were carried out in two villages, namely; Ngadisari and Sapikerep villages of Probolinggo. This study uses representation theory in order to explain in more detail how Tengger people strategy to present their identity in a midst of the struggle to face the impact of changes in this time. The dialectics between the struggle for survival of Tengger community against power dynamics of (Islam) have led to two important points; firstly, that the Tengger custom and culture have made Hindu and Islamic ideology as a cultural system which is merely symbolically attached to Tengger. Secondly, that although being impressed with syncretic characteristics, Islam and Hinduism can build a worldview for the Tengger community in a way which is considered to be substantial and highly valued culturally. This view is as common as Islam in Java called "Javanese Islam" as a character behind syncretic trends, but in the sense that it presents "substantial Islam", hence it is based on religious traditions of Sufism.
